---
title: "Fimo results plotting"
output: html_document
date: "2025-11-05"
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

# Preparation

## Working directory
```{r}
working.dir <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment"

setwd(working.dir)
```

## Libraries
```{r}
suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(tidyverse)
  library(rtracklayer)
  library(Biostrings)    
  library(biomaRt)
  library(universalmotif)
  library(tidyverse)
  library(gridExtra)
})
```

# Fimo results

## De novo motifs

### Load data
```{r}

gff_file <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment/7-fimo/fimo_motifs_deduplicated__on__actin_vs_no_infect_scramble_sig_enrich_3pSeqs/fimo.gff"
fa_file  <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment/7-fimo/actin_vs_no_infect_scramble_sig_enrich_3pSeqs.fasta"

gr <- rtracklayer::import(gff_file)

fa <- tryCatch(Biostrings::readDNAStringSet(fa_file), error = function(e) NULL)
if (is.null(fa)) fa <- Biostrings::readAAStringSet(fa_file)
```

### Plot results
```{r}
# GFF -> tidy
gff <- as_tibble(gr) %>%
  transmute(
    seq_id = as.character(sub("\\|.*", "", seqnames)),
    start  = start,
    end    = end,
    strand = as.character(strand),
    motif  = if ("Name" %in% names(mcols(gr))) as.character(sub("\\_.*", "", Name)) else type,
    score  = if ("score" %in% colnames(.)) score else NA_real_
  )

hdr <- names(fa)

seq_lengths <- tibble(
  seq_id = sub("\\|.*", "", hdr),
  transcript_id = sub("^[^|]*\\|(?:transcript:)?([^|]+).*", "\\1", hdr, perl = TRUE),
  strand = sub(".*\\|strand:([+-]).*", "\\1", hdr),
  seqlen = width(fa)
)

# get gene symbol
mart <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "mmusculus_gene_ensembl")
gene_map <- getBM(
  attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id", "mgi_symbol"),
  filters = "ensembl_gene_id",
  values = unique(sub("\\|.*", "", names(fa))),
  mart = mart
) %>%
  rename(seq_id = ensembl_gene_id, gene_symbol = mgi_symbol)

seq_lengths <- seq_lengths %>%
  left_join(gene_map,
            by = "seq_id")

# Order: longest first, promote target to top, then reverse for plotting (so first is at top)
target <- "ENSMUSG00000029580"
lvl_by_len <- seq_lengths %>% arrange(desc(seqlen)) %>% pull(seq_id)
lvl <- rev(c(target, setdiff(lvl_by_len, target)))

# Shared factor + numeric y for all layers
seq_levels <- factor(seq_lengths$seq_id, levels = lvl)
y_lookup <- tibble(seq_id = levels(seq_levels)) %>% mutate(y = row_number())

# Join lengths + y to hits; expand width for visibility
expand_width <- 20
hits <- gff %>%
  inner_join(seq_lengths, by = "seq_id") %>%
  inner_join(y_lookup, by = "seq_id") %>%
  mutate(
    xmin = pmax(1L, start - expand_width/2),
    xmax = pmin(seqlen, end   + expand_width/2)
  )

# Baselines in same tibble
baselines <- seq_lengths %>%
  inner_join(y_lookup, by = "seq_id") %>%
  transmute(
    type = "baseline",
    seq_id, seqlen, y,
    x0 = 1L, x1 = seqlen,
    xmin = NA_real_, xmax = NA_real_, motif = NA_character_
  )

# Motifs present in the target sequence
target_motifs <- hits %>%
  filter(seq_id == target) %>%
  pull(motif) %>%
  unique()

# Keep only hits whose motif is among target motifs
hits <- hits %>%
  filter(motif %in% target_motifs)

# (Optional) drop sequences with no remaining hits
seq_with_hits <- hits %>% distinct(seq_id)
baselines <- baselines %>%
  semi_join(seq_with_hits, by = "seq_id")

# Hits in same tibble
hits_df <- hits %>%
  transmute(
    type = "hit",
    seq_id, seqlen, y, x0 = NA_real_, x1 = NA_real_,
    xmin, xmax, motif, transcript_id, gene_symbol
  )

df_plot <- bind_rows(baselines, hits_df)

# Give motif names
motif_ids <- unique(df_plot$motif[!is.na(df_plot$motif)])
motif_labels <- paste0("Motif ", seq_along(motif_ids), " (", motif_ids, ")")
names(motif_labels) <- motif_ids
df_plot$motif <- factor(df_plot$motif,
                        levels = motif_ids)

# build labels from df_plot (use first non-NA from any hit; fallback to seq_id)
spacing_factor <- 0.5   # smaller = tighter spacing (try 0.5–0.7)
df_plot <- df_plot %>%
  mutate(y = y * spacing_factor)

# Then regenerate y_breaks/y_labels using the updated df_plot
lab_tbl <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(seq_id, y) %>%
  summarise(
    gene_symbol   = first(na.omit(gene_symbol)),
    transcript_id = first(na.omit(transcript_id)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(label = paste0(gene_symbol, " (", transcript_id, ")")) %>%
  arrange(y)

y_breaks <- lab_tbl$y
y_labels <- lab_tbl$label

# ---- color mapping -------------------------------------------------------
# If you have exactly 3 motifs and want cyan/blue/red like the example:
named_cols <- c("#6DE1E8", "#1F4BE3", "#D34A3A")
motifs_unique <- unique(hits$motif)
pal <- setNames(
  if (length(motifs_unique) <= 3) named_cols[seq_along(motifs_unique)]
  else scales::hue_pal()(length(motifs_unique)),
  motifs_unique
)

# Keep only seqs that are actually present (hit or baseline) and re-densify y
y_map <- df_plot %>%
  distinct(seq_id) %>%                     # only what’s left
  arrange(factor(seq_id, levels = unique(df_plot$seq_id[order(df_plot$y)]))) %>%
  mutate(y_dense = row_number())

# Apply compact y to everything
df_plot <- df_plot %>% select(-y) %>% left_join(y_map, by = "seq_id") %>% rename(y = y_dense)

# Rebuild axis labels from your metadata
lab_tbl <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(seq_id, y) %>%
  summarise(
    gene_symbol   = first(na.omit(gene_symbol)),
    transcript_id = first(na.omit(transcript_id)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(label = ifelse(is.na(transcript_id),
                        gene_symbol,
                        paste0(gene_symbol, " (", transcript_id, ")"))) %>%
  arrange(y)

y_breaks <- lab_tbl$y
y_labels <- lab_tbl$label

# Plot (both layers from the same df)
box_half_height <- 0.3

pdf("motif_mapping.pdf",
    width = 7,
    height = 3)
ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = df_plot %>% filter(type=="baseline"),
    aes(x = x0, xend = x1, y = y, yend = y),
    color = "grey80", linewidth = 1, lineend = "round"
  ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = df_plot %>% filter(type == "hit"),
    aes(
      xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax,
      ymin = y - box_half_height, ymax = y + box_half_height,
      fill = motif
    ),
    color = NA
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = NULL, labels = motif_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = y_breaks, labels = y_labels) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_scientific(),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, max(df_plot$seqlen, na.rm = TRUE))) +
  labs(x = "Position in 3'UTR (bp)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
theme(
  text = element_text(color = "black"),
  axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
  axis.title = element_text(color = "black"),
  legend.text = element_text(color = "black"),
  legend.title = element_text(color = "black"),
  panel.grid = element_blank(),
  axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
  legend.position = "none",
  axis.line.x = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.4)
)
dev.off()

pdf("motif_mapping_legend.pdf",
    width = 7,
    height = 3)
ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = df_plot %>% filter(type=="baseline"),
    aes(x = x0, xend = x1, y = y, yend = y),
    color = "grey80", linewidth = 1, lineend = "round"
  ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = df_plot %>% filter(type == "hit"),
    aes(
      xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax,
      ymin = y - box_half_height, ymax = y + box_half_height,
      fill = motif
    ),
    color = NA
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = NULL, labels = motif_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = y_breaks, labels = y_labels) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_scientific(),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, max(df_plot$seqlen, na.rm = TRUE))) +
  labs(x = "Position in 3'UTR (bp)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
theme(
  text = element_text(color = "black"),
  axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
  axis.title = element_text(color = "black"),
  legend.text = element_text(color = "black"),
  legend.title = element_text(color = "black"),
  panel.grid = element_blank(),
  axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
  legend.position = "right"
)
dev.off()
```


## oRNAment motfis
### Load data
```{r}

gff_file <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment/7-fimo/fimo_oRNAment_actb_rbp__on__actin_vs_no_infect_scramble_sig_enrich_3pSeqs/fimo.gff"
fa_file  <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment/7-fimo/actin_vs_no_infect_scramble_sig_enrich_3pSeqs.fasta"

gr <- rtracklayer::import(gff_file)

fa <- tryCatch(Biostrings::readDNAStringSet(fa_file), error = function(e) NULL)
if (is.null(fa)) fa <- Biostrings::readAAStringSet(fa_file)
```

### Plot results
```{r}
# GFF -> tidy
gff <- as_tibble(gr) %>%
  transmute(
    seq_id = as.character(sub("\\|.*", "", seqnames)),
    start  = start,
    end    = end,
    strand = as.character(strand),
    motif  = if ("Name" %in% names(mcols(gr))) as.character(sub("\\_.*", "", Name)) else type,
    score  = if ("score" %in% colnames(.)) score else NA_real_
  )

hdr <- names(fa)

seq_lengths <- tibble(
  seq_id = sub("\\|.*", "", hdr),
  transcript_id = sub("^[^|]*\\|(?:transcript:)?([^|]+).*", "\\1", hdr, perl = TRUE),
  strand = sub(".*\\|strand:([+-]).*", "\\1", hdr),
  seqlen = width(fa)
)

# get gene symbol
mart <- useMart("ensembl", dataset = "mmusculus_gene_ensembl")
gene_map <- getBM(
  attributes = c("ensembl_gene_id", "mgi_symbol"),
  filters = "ensembl_gene_id",
  values = unique(sub("\\|.*", "", names(fa))),
  mart = mart
) %>%
  rename(seq_id = ensembl_gene_id, gene_symbol = mgi_symbol)

seq_lengths <- seq_lengths %>%
  left_join(gene_map,
            by = "seq_id")

# Order: longest first, promote target to top, then reverse for plotting (so first is at top)
target <- "ENSMUSG00000029580"
lvl_by_len <- seq_lengths %>% arrange(desc(seqlen)) %>% pull(seq_id)
lvl <- rev(c(target, setdiff(lvl_by_len, target)))

# Shared factor + numeric y for all layers
seq_levels <- factor(seq_lengths$seq_id, levels = lvl)
y_lookup <- tibble(seq_id = levels(seq_levels)) %>% mutate(y = row_number())

# Join lengths + y to hits; expand width for visibility
expand_width <- 20
hits <- gff %>%
  inner_join(seq_lengths, by = "seq_id") %>%
  inner_join(y_lookup, by = "seq_id") %>%
  mutate(
    xmin = pmax(1L, start - expand_width/2),
    xmax = pmin(seqlen, end   + expand_width/2)
  )

# Baselines in same tibble
baselines <- seq_lengths %>%
  inner_join(y_lookup, by = "seq_id") %>%
  transmute(
    type = "baseline",
    seq_id, seqlen, y,
    x0 = 1L, x1 = seqlen,
    xmin = NA_real_, xmax = NA_real_, motif = NA_character_
  )

# Motifs present in the target sequence
target_motifs <- hits %>%
  filter(seq_id == target) %>%
  pull(motif) %>%
  unique()

# Keep only hits whose motif is among target motifs
hits <- hits %>%
  filter(motif %in% target_motifs)

# (Optional) drop sequences with no remaining hits
seq_with_hits <- hits %>% distinct(seq_id)
baselines <- baselines %>%
  semi_join(seq_with_hits, by = "seq_id")

# Hits in same tibble
hits_df <- hits %>%
  transmute(
    type = "hit",
    seq_id, seqlen, y, x0 = NA_real_, x1 = NA_real_,
    xmin, xmax, motif, transcript_id, gene_symbol
  )

df_plot <- bind_rows(baselines, hits_df)

# Give motif names
motif_ids <- unique(df_plot$motif[!is.na(df_plot$motif)])
motif_labels <- paste0("Motif ", seq_along(motif_ids), " (", motif_ids, ")")
names(motif_labels) <- motif_ids
df_plot$motif <- factor(df_plot$motif,
                        levels = motif_ids)

# build labels from df_plot (use first non-NA from any hit; fallback to seq_id)
spacing_factor <- 0.5   # smaller = tighter spacing (try 0.5–0.7)
df_plot <- df_plot %>%
  mutate(y = y * spacing_factor)

# Then regenerate y_breaks/y_labels using the updated df_plot
lab_tbl <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(seq_id, y) %>%
  summarise(
    gene_symbol   = first(na.omit(gene_symbol)),
    transcript_id = first(na.omit(transcript_id)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(label = paste0(gene_symbol, " (", transcript_id, ")")) %>%
  arrange(y)

y_breaks <- lab_tbl$y
y_labels <- lab_tbl$label

# ---- color mapping -------------------------------------------------------
# If you have exactly 3 motifs and want cyan/blue/red like the example:
named_cols <- c("#6DE1E8", "#1F4BE3", "#D34A3A")
motifs_unique <- unique(hits$motif)
pal <- setNames(
  if (length(motifs_unique) <= 3) named_cols[seq_along(motifs_unique)]
  else scales::hue_pal()(length(motifs_unique)),
  motifs_unique
)

# Keep only seqs that are actually present (hit or baseline) and re-densify y
y_map <- df_plot %>%
  distinct(seq_id) %>%                     # only what’s left
  arrange(factor(seq_id, levels = unique(df_plot$seq_id[order(df_plot$y)]))) %>%
  mutate(y_dense = row_number())

# Apply compact y to everything
df_plot <- df_plot %>% select(-y) %>% left_join(y_map, by = "seq_id") %>% rename(y = y_dense)

# Rebuild axis labels from your metadata
lab_tbl <- df_plot %>%
  group_by(seq_id, y) %>%
  summarise(
    gene_symbol   = first(na.omit(gene_symbol)),
    transcript_id = first(na.omit(transcript_id)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(label = ifelse(is.na(transcript_id),
                        gene_symbol,
                        paste0(gene_symbol, " (", transcript_id, ")"))) %>%
  arrange(y)

y_breaks <- lab_tbl$y
y_labels <- lab_tbl$label

# Plot (both layers from the same df)
box_half_height <- 0.3

pdf("oRNAment_motif_mapping.pdf",
    width = 7,
    height = 3)
ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = df_plot %>% filter(type=="baseline"),
    aes(x = x0, xend = x1, y = y, yend = y),
    color = "grey80", linewidth = 1, lineend = "round"
  ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = df_plot %>% filter(type == "hit"),
    aes(
      xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax,
      ymin = y - box_half_height, ymax = y + box_half_height,
      fill = motif
    ),
    color = NA
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = NULL, labels = motif_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = y_breaks, labels = y_labels) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_scientific(),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, max(df_plot$seqlen, na.rm = TRUE))) +
  labs(x = "Position in 3'UTR (bp)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
theme(
  text = element_text(color = "black"),
  axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
  axis.title = element_text(color = "black"),
  legend.text = element_text(color = "black"),
  legend.title = element_text(color = "black"),
  panel.grid = element_blank(),
  axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
  legend.position = "none",
  axis.line.x = element_line(color = "black", linewidth = 0.4)
)
dev.off()

pdf("oRNAment_motif_mapping_legend.pdf",
    width = 7,
    height = 5)
ggplot() +
  geom_segment(
    data = df_plot %>% filter(type=="baseline"),
    aes(x = x0, xend = x1, y = y, yend = y),
    color = "grey80", linewidth = 1, lineend = "round"
  ) +
  geom_rect(
    data = df_plot %>% filter(type == "hit"),
    aes(
      xmin = xmin, xmax = xmax,
      ymin = y - box_half_height, ymax = y + box_half_height,
      fill = motif
    ),
    color = NA
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = pal, name = NULL, labels = motif_labels) +
  scale_y_continuous(breaks = y_breaks, labels = y_labels) +
  scale_x_continuous(
    labels = scales::label_scientific(),
    expand = c(0, 0)
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, max(df_plot$seqlen, na.rm = TRUE))) +
  labs(x = "Position in 3'UTR (bp)", y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
theme(
  text = element_text(color = "black"),
  axis.text = element_text(color = "black"),
  axis.title = element_text(color = "black"),
  legend.text = element_text(color = "black"),
  legend.title = element_text(color = "black"),
  panel.grid = element_blank(),
  axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
  legend.position = "right"
)
dev.off()
```

# STREME results de novo motif

## Load data
```{r}
# Path to your motif file (can be MEME, JASPAR, etc.)
motif_file <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment/5-consensus/motifs_deduplicated.meme"

# ---- read motifs ----
motifs <- read_meme(motif_file)
```

## Plot results
```{r}
# 'motifs' is a list of universalmotif objects
length(motifs)        # how many motifs found
motifs[[1]]           # inspect first motif

# Step 1: filter motifs to only those in motif_labels (by motif name)
motifs_filt <- Filter(function(m) m@name %in% names(motif_labels), motifs)

# Step 2: order them to match the order in motif_labels
motifs_filt <- motifs_filt[match(names(motif_labels),
                                 vapply(motifs_filt, slot, "", "name"))]

# Step 3: rename motif objects to have the pretty labels
for (i in seq_along(motifs_filt)) {
  motifs_filt[[i]]@name <- motif_labels[names(motif_labels)[i]]
}

vapply(motifs_filt, slot, "", "name")

# ---- plot motifs ----
view_motifs(motifs_filt[[1]],
            )

for (i in seq_along(motifs_filt)) {
  m <- motifs_filt[[i]]
  
  # Get current name and E-value
  nm <- m@name
  ev <- tryCatch(slot(m, "eval"), error = function(e) NA_real_)
  
  # Format the E-value nicely in scientific notation
  ev_label <- if (is.na(ev)) "E=NA" else paste0("E=", formatC(ev, format = "e", digits = 2))
  
  # Update the motif name to include E-value
  m@name <- paste0(nm, "  ", ev_label)
  
  motifs_filt[[i]] <- m
}

vapply(motifs_filt, slot, "", "name")

# plot aligned motifs
pdf("motif_streme_results.pdf",
    height = 5,
    width = 5)
view_motifs(motifs_filt,
            show.names = TRUE,
            names.pos	= "top",
            use.type = "ICM",
            tryRC = FALSE)
dev.off()
```

# TOMTOM results de novo vs oRNAment motifs

## Load data
```{r}
# ---- inputs ----
meme_query <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment/6-tomtom/oRNAment_actb/motifs_deduplicated.meme"
meme_db    <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment/6-tomtom/oRNAment_actb/oRNAment_actb_rbp.meme"
tomtom_tsv <- "~/EinsteinMed Dropbox/Ronald Cutler/Vijg-lab/Collaborations/Jackson Rogow/transcriptomics/analysis/with-virus-and-stem-loop/batch-2-actin/motif_enrichment/6-tomtom/oRNAment_actb/tomtom_motifs_deduplicated/tomtom.tsv"

# ---- load motifs ----
Q <- read_meme(meme_query)  # list of universalmotif objects
D <- read_meme(meme_db)
```

## Plot results
```{r}
# fast name → index lookups (assumes Tomtom IDs match MEME motif names)
q_names <- vapply(Q, function(m) m@name, character(1))
d_names <- vapply(D, function(m) m@name, character(1))
q_index <- setNames(seq_along(Q), q_names)
d_index <- setNames(seq_along(D), d_names)

# ---- read tomtom results (ignore header comments) ----
tt <- read_tsv(tomtom_tsv, comment = "#", show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
  # keep columns we need; adjust thresholds as you like
  mutate(
    Optimal_offset = as.integer(Optimal_offset),
    Orientation = as.character(Orientation)
  )

# ---- helper: fetch motif by ID with a small fallback ----
get_motif <- function(motif_list, idx_map, id) {
  if (!is.na(idx_map[id])) return(motif_list[[ idx_map[id] ]])
  # fallback: try stripping spaces or parentheses quirks
  id2 <- str_replace_all(id, "\\s+", "")
  id2 <- str_replace_all(id2, "\\(\\d+\\)$", "")
  hits <- which(str_replace_all(vapply(motif_list, function(m) m@name, ""), "\\s+", "") == id2)
  if (length(hits)) motif_list[[ hits[1] ]] else stop("Motif not found: ", id)
}

# ---- helper: label + optional reverse complement for target ----
prep_pair <- function(row) {
  E  <- as.numeric(row[["E-value"]])
  mq <- get_motif(Q, q_index, row[["Query_ID"]])
  mt <- get_motif(D, d_index, row[["Target_ID"]])
  mq@name <- sprintf("%s | E=%.3g",
                     mq@name, E)
  list(query = mq, target = mt)
}

# pair 1
pdf("ACCUAAA_oRNAment_match.pdf",
    width = 4,
    height = 3)
view_motifs(prep_pair(sig[1, ]))
dev.off()

# pair 2
pdf("UGACAAA_oRNAment_match.pdf",
    width = 4,
    height = 3)
view_motifs(prep_pair(sig[2, ]))
dev.off()

# pair 3
pdf("GACAGCAGAU_oRNAment_match.pdf",
    width = 4,
    height = 3)
view_motifs(prep_pair(sig[3, ]))
dev.off()
```
